reasoning: There is an FOMC meeting scheduled for March 17-18, 2026, falling securely within the resolution window (March 15 to March 22). As of March 2026, the CME FedWatch Tool forecasts a 92%+ probability that the Fed will hold the federal funds rate steady (the current lower limit is 3.500%). The remainder of market expectations heavily favor eventual rate cuts rather than hikes, aligning with the FOMC's late 2025 dot plot which projected one or two cuts in 2026. Therefore, the probability of the Fed fundamentally reversing course with a surprise rate *increase* at the March meeting is virtually zero. I am estimating at the lowest allowable probability bound for this framework.
